entries.title: Supply and Demand
entries.content: Price equilibrium occurs where quantity supplied equals quantity demanded. Higher demand raises price; higher supply lowers price (other factors equal).

entries.title: GDP Components
entries.content: Gross Domestic Product measures total output: GDP = Consumption + Investment + Government spending + (Exports − Imports).

entries.title: Inflation
entries.content: Sustained rise in general price level. Measured by CPI. Central banks raise interest rates to cool inflation; lower rates to stimulate economy.

entries.title: Opportunity Cost
entries.content: The value of the next-best alternative forgone when making a choice. Core concept in economics and decision-making.

entries.title: Comparative Advantage
entries.content: Countries benefit from trade by specializing in goods they produce relatively more efficiently, even if one country is better at everything.
